message:
test(ntm-driver): Add ShellCheck disable directives (bd-m3a5)

Add SC2034 (variables used by sourced functions) and SC2123 (intentional
PATH modification for testing) to fix ShellCheck warnings.

32 tests, 48 assertions all passing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-kzzl","title":"Write comprehensive preflight check tests","description":"# Comprehensive Preflight Check Tests

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_preflight_checks.sh

## Purpose
Verify all 13 preflight conditions work correctly. Each check must pass before invoking the agent.

## Preflight Conditions to Test (13 total)

1. **is_git_repo** - Directory is a git repository
2. **git_email_configured** - user.email is set (ADDED)
3. **git_name_configured** - user.name is set (ADDED)
4. **not_shallow_clone** - Repository is not a shallow clone (ADDED)
5. **no_dirty_submodules** - Submodules are clean (ADDED)
6. **no_rebase_in_progress** - .git/rebase-apply or rebase-merge absent
7. **no_merge_in_progress** - .git/MERGE_HEAD absent
8. **no_cherry_pick_in_progress** - .git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD absent
9. **not_detached_HEAD** - symbolic-ref returns valid branch
10. **has_upstream** - @{u} exists (when push strategy != none)
11. **not_diverged** - not both ahead AND behind upstream
12. **no_unmerged_paths** - no merge conflicts
13. **diff_check_clean** - no whitespace errors
14. **reasonable_untracked_count** - not too many untracked files

## Test Functions

### Git Identity Tests (NEW)
```bash
test_preflight_git_identity() {
    log_test_start "Git identity configuration check"

    local repo=$(mktemp -d)
    git init "$repo" >/dev/null 2>&1

    log_verbose "Testing with no identity"
    git -C "$repo" config --unset user.email 2>/dev/null || true
    repo_preflight_check "$repo" && fail "Should fail without email"
    assert_equals "git_email_not_configured" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    log_verbose "Testing with email but no name"
    git -C "$repo" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$repo" config --unset user.name 2>/dev/null || true
    repo_preflight_check "$repo" && fail "Should fail without name"
    assert_equals "git_name_not_configured" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    rm -rf "$repo"
    log_success "Git identity tests passed"
}
```

### Shallow Clone Test (NEW)
```bash
test_preflight_shallow_clone() {
    log_test_start "Shallow clone detection"

    # Create a repo with history
    local origin=$(mktemp -d)
    git init "$origin" >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$origin" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$origin" config user.name "Test"
    echo "file" > "$origin/file.txt"
    git -C "$origin" add . && git -C "$origin" commit -m "init" >/dev/null
    echo "file2" > "$origin/file2.txt"
    git -C "$origin" add . && git -C "$origin" commit -m "second" >/dev/null

    # Shallow clone
    local shallow=$(mktemp -d)
    git clone --depth 1 "file://$origin" "$shallow" >/dev/null 2>&1

    log_verbose "Testing shallow clone detection"
    [[ -f "$shallow/.git/shallow" ]] || fail "Test setup: should be shallow"
    repo_preflight_check "$shallow" && fail "Should reject shallow clone"
    assert_equals "shallow_clone" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    rm -rf "$origin" "$shallow"
    log_success "Shallow clone tests passed"
}
```

### Submodule Test (NEW)
```bash
test_preflight_dirty_submodules() {
    log_test_start "Dirty submodule detection"

    # Setup: parent repo with submodule
    local submod=$(mktemp -d)
    local parent=$(mktemp -d)

    git init "$submod" >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$submod" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$submod" config user.name "Test"
    echo "sub" > "$submod/sub.txt"
    git -C "$submod" add . && git -C "$submod" commit -m "init" >/dev/null

    git init "$parent" >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$parent" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$parent" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$parent" submodule add "file://$submod" sub >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$parent" commit -m "add submodule" >/dev/null

    log_verbose "Testing clean submodule"
    repo_preflight_check "$parent" || fail "Should pass with clean submodule"

    log_verbose "Testing dirty submodule"
    echo "dirty" > "$parent/sub/dirty.txt"
    repo_preflight_check "$parent" && fail "Should reject dirty submodule"
    assert_equals "dirty_submodules" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    rm -rf "$submod" "$parent"
    log_success "Submodule tests passed"
}
```

## Parallel Preflight Tests
```bash
test_parallel_preflight() {
    log_test_start "Parallel preflight execution"

    # Create mix of valid and invalid repos
    local valid1=$(mktemp -d)
    local valid2=$(mktemp -d)
    local invalid=$(mktemp -d)

    setup_valid_repo "$valid1"
    setup_valid_repo "$valid2"
    # Don't init invalid - it's not a git repo

    local repos=("$valid1" "$valid2" "$invalid")

    run_parallel_preflight repos

    assert_equals 2 "${#repos[@]}" "Should have 2 valid repos"
    assert_contains "${repos[*]}" "$valid1" "Should include valid1"
    assert_contains "${repos[*]}" "$valid2" "Should include valid2"

    rm -rf "$valid1" "$valid2" "$invalid"
    log_success "Parallel preflight tests passed"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 preflight conditions have dedicated tests
- [ ] Tests create realistic scenarios (actual git repos)
- [ ] Failure messages include skip reasons
- [ ] Tests clean up temp directories
- [ ] Parallel preflight tested with mix of repos
- [ ] Verbose logging shows test progress

 {"id":"bd-ldyv","title":"Implement quality gates framework (tests/lint before push)","description":"Task: Implement Quality Gates Framework

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

Policy Configuration
--------------------
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:

REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS=true
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true

Gate Results in Plan
--------------------
Update plan with gate results:
{
  "git": {
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  }
}

Testing
-------
- Verify auto-detection works
- Verify configured commands run
- Verify failures block push
- Verify secret scan catches patterns
- Test with missing tools

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Test gate runs before push
- [ ] Lint gate runs if configured
- [ ] Secret scan warns on patterns
- [ ] Failures block push with message
- [ ] Per-repo policies work
- [ ] Auto-detection works for common projects","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:21:11.827464852-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:21:11.827464852-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented quality gates framework with 8 functions: detect_test_command, detect_lint_command, run_test_gate, run_lint_gate, run_secret_scan, run_quality_gates, update_plan_with_gates. Supports auto-detection for Makefile, npm, Cargo, Python, Go, shell. Integrates with per-repo policies.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.491001528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-cutq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:10:25.431274346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File

## Tests to Implement

### JSON Parsing Tests
```bash
test_json_get_field() {
    local json="{\"success\":true,\"error\":\"test error\"}"
    local result
    result=$(json_get_field "$json" "error")
    assert_equals "test error" "$result" "Should extract error field"
}

test_json_is_success() {
    json_is_success "{\"success\":true}"
    assert_equals 0 $? "Should return 0 for success:true"
    
    json_is_success "{\"success\":false}"
    assert_equals 1 $? "Should return 1 for success:false"
}

test_json_escape() {
    local result=$(json_escape "quote\"and\\backslash")
    assert_equals "quote\\\"and\\\\backslash" "$result"
}
```

### ntm Check Tests
```bash
test_ntm_check_available_not_installed() {
    PATH="/empty" ntm_check_available
    assert_equals 1 $? "Should return 1 when ntm not installed"
}
```

### Preflight Tests
```bash
test_has_uncommitted_changes() {
    local test_repo=$(mktemp -d)
    git -C "$test_repo" init
    
    has_uncommitted_changes "$test_repo"
    assert_equals 1 $? "Clean repo should return 1"
    
    touch "$test_repo/newfile"
    has_uncommitted_changes "$test_repo"
    assert_equals 0 $? "Dirty repo should return 0"
    
    rm -rf "$test_repo"
}
```

### Security Tests
```bash
test_is_file_denied() {
    source "$RU_SCRIPT"
    
    is_file_denied ".env"
    assert_equals 0 $? ".env should be denied"
    
    is_file_denied "src/main.py"
    assert_equals 1 $? "src/main.py should be allowed"
}
```

 {"id":"bd-maf5","title":"Implement user-friendly error messages for agent-sweep","description":"Implements clear, actionable error messages for all agent-sweep failure modes.

## Background

When agent-sweep fails, users need to understand:
1. What went wrong
2. Why it happened
3. What they can do to fix it

This task ensures all error paths produce helpful, consistent messages.

## Error Categories and Messages

### 1. Preflight Failures
```
ERROR: Cannot run agent-sweep on owner/repo
  Reason: Repository has uncommitted changes
  
  To fix:
    cd /path/to/repo
    git stash       # Save changes temporarily
    ru agent-sweep  # Run sweep
    git stash pop   # Restore changes
```

### 2. NTM Errors
```
ERROR: Failed to start AI agent session
  Reason: ntm returned error: "no API key configured"
  
  To fix:
    Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY is set
    Run: ntm doctor
```

### 3. Agent Timeouts
```
WARNING: Agent timed out during Phase 2 for owner/repo
  The agent did not complete within 300 seconds.
  
  Options:
    --timeout 600    # Increase timeout
    --retry          # Retry failed repos
    --skip owner/repo  # Skip this repo
```

### 4. Validation Failures
```
ERROR: Commit plan validation failed for owner/repo
  Issues found:
    - Line 42: File matches denylist: .env.production
    - Line 58: File exceeds size limit: large-binary.bin (15MB > 10MB)
  
  The agent proposed changes that violate security rules.
  These files will not be committed.
```

### 5. Rate Limits
```
WARNING: API rate limit reached
  Backing off for 60 seconds before retry...
  
  Progress will resume automatically.
  Press Ctrl+C to interrupt (state will be saved).
```

## Implementation

### Error Formatting Function

```bash
format_error() {
  local category="$1" reason="$2" fix="$3"
  echo "" >&2
  echo "ERROR: $category" >&2
  echo "  Reason: $reason" >&2
  if [[ -n "$fix" ]]; then
    echo "" >&2
    echo "  To fix:" >&2
    echo "$fix" | sed "s/^/    /" >&2
  fi
  echo "" >&2
}
```

### Exit Code Mapping

Map each error category to appropriate exit code per ru conventions:
- 1: Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2: Conflicts (validation issues)
- 3: Dependency error (ntm not available)
- 4: Invalid arguments
- 5: Interrupted (user Ctrl+C)

## Related Beads

- Part of: bd-kvu5 (Error Handling & Recovery)
- Works with: bd-mrb2 (verbose logging)
- Works with: bd-rhea (summary printing)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All error paths produce formatted, helpful messages
- [ ] Messages include actionable fix suggestions
- [ ] Exit codes match ru conventions
- [ ] Errors distinguish between recoverable and fatal
- [ ] Multi-repo errors show per-repo details in summary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:23:06.781946345-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:03:34.397597191-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:03:34.397597191-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented user-friendly error messages with format_agent_sweep_error/warning and 13 category-specific helpers"}
 {"id":"bd-mcvj","title":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing","description":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing

Overview
--------
Security safeguards, analytics collection, and comprehensive testing
to ensure the review system is safe, measurable, and reliable.

Components
----------

7.1 Security: Command Validation
   Prevent dangerous commands:
   - Blocked list: sudo, rm -rf, eval
   - gh mutation blocking in Plan mode
   - Approval required for risky ops
   - Pre-exec hook validation

7.2 Security: Secret Scanning
   Detect secrets before push:
   - gitleaks integration
   - Regex fallback patterns
   - Block push on detection
   - Human review option

7.3 Metrics Collection
   Track review analytics:
   - Reviews per period
   - Issues resolved
   - Questions asked/answered
   - Time per repo
   - Decision breakdown

7.4 Decision Tracking
   Learn from past decisions:
   - Record decision patterns
   - Suggest based on history
   - Per-repo preferences
   - JSONL format for analysis

7.5 Analytics Dashboard
   Visualize review effectiveness:
   - Overview stats
   - Decision breakdown chart
   - Top repos by activity
   - Efficiency trend

7.6 Unit Tests
   Test individual functions:
   - GraphQL batching
   - Priority scoring
   - JSON parsing
   - State management

7.7 Integration Tests
   Test full workflows:
   - Discovery to apply
   - Question handling
   - Error recovery
   - Resume functionality

7.8 Test Fixtures
   Mock data for testing:
   - Claude stream examples
   - GraphQL responses
   - Review plan examples
   - Error scenarios

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Security controls prevent dangerous ops
- Metrics collected accurately
- Analytics dashboard works
- Unit test coverage adequate
- Integration tests pass
- Fixtures enable reliable testing

Estimated Effort
----------------
~500 lines (security/metrics)

 {"id":"bd-mkoc","title":"[EPIC] Agent Sweep Command Implementation","description":"# Agent Sweep Command Implementation

## Purpose
Implement the main `ru agent-sweep` command that orchestrates AI-assisted repository maintenance.

## Command Syntax
ru agent-sweep [options]

## Core Workflow
1. Check ntm availability (fail fast if missing)
2. Load all repos from ~/.config/ru/repos.d/*.txt
3. Filter to repos with uncommitted changes (git status --porcelain)
4. Run preflight safety checks on each repo
5. Process repos (sequential or parallel)
6. For each repo: spawn→send Phase 1→wait→send Phase 2→wait→(optionally Phase 3)→cleanup
7. Produce summary report

## Key Options
- --with-release: Enable Phase 3 (release workflow)
- -j N, --parallel=N: Process N repos concurrently
- --repos=PATTERN: Filter repos by pattern
- --dry-run: Show what would be processed
- --resume / --restart: State recovery
- --phase{1,2,3}-timeout=N: Phase-specific timeouts
- --execution-mode=MODE: plan|apply|agent (default: apply)
- --keep-sessions / --keep-sessions-on-fail: Session preservation
- --release-strategy=STR: never|auto|tag-only|gh-release
- --secret-scan=MODE: auto|on|off

## Exit Codes
- 0: All repos processed successfully
- 1: Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2: Conflicts (unresolved issues)
- 3: Dependency error (ntm/tmux not available)
- 4: Invalid arguments
- 5: Interrupted (use --resume)

## Session Naming Convention
ru_sweep_{repo_name_sanitized}_{pid}[_{worker_index}]

## Related Code
- Reuses existing load_all_repos() pattern
- Reuses parallel sync pattern from run_parallel_sync()
- Matches existing CLI argument parsing style","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:45:17.136816673-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:45:17.136816673-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mkoc","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.068499268-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mkoc","depends_on_id":"bd-9o2h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:27.475267953-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mkoc","depends_on_id":"bd-jk4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:27.501227978-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mkoc","depends_on_id":"bd-cpxq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:27.523631558-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mkoc","depends_on_id":"bd-1vfe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:27.54460244-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

End-to-end tests for the clone driver subsystem.

3. Clone with depth limiting (shallow clone)
4. Clone with branch specification
5. Clone failure and retry behavior
6. Clone rate limiting respect

## Requirements
- Real network operations to test repositories
- JSON logging: url, method, attempt, duration_ms, bytes_transferred, result
- Network failure simulation via test hooks
- Verify cloned repository integrity (git fsck)

- [ ] Retry logic validated with configurable delays
- [ ] Clean error messages for common failures","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:56:37.660060087-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T14:22:48.151527534-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T14:22:48.151527534-05:00","close_reason":"Added 5 new E2E tests for clone driver: branch specification, failure handling, timeout simulation, dry-run verification, and parallel execution.  {"id":"bd-mnu9","title":"Add cmd_review() command skeleton with argument parsing","description":"# Task: Add cmd_review() Command Skeleton

## Purpose
Create the entry point for all review functionality - the cmd_review() function that will orchestrate the entire review workflow.

## Implementation Details

### Location in ru script
Add after cmd_prune() (around line 3634), following existing command pattern.

### Function Structure
```bash
cmd_review() {
    local mode="auto"
    local parallel=4
    local dry_run="false"
    local resume="false"
    local apply="false"
    local push="false"
    local priority_threshold="all"
    local max_repos=""
    local max_runtime=""
    local max_questions=""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_review_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_review_prerequisites || exit 3

    # Generate unique run ID
    REVIEW_RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"

    # Acquire global lock
    acquire_review_lock || { log_error "Another review is running"; exit 1; }

    # Auto-detect driver
    [[ "$mode" == "auto" ]] && mode=$(detect_review_driver)

    # Discovery phase
    log_step "Scanning repositories for open issues and PRs..."
    local -a work_items
    discover_work_items work_items "$priority_threshold" "$max_repos"

    if [[ ${#work_items[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No work items need review"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # Show summary
    show_discovery_summary "${work_items[@]}"

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Dry run - exiting without starting sessions"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # ... orchestration phases follow
}
```

### Arguments to Support
| Argument | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| --plan | (default) | Generate plans only, no mutations |
| --apply | false | Execute approved plans |
| --mode=MODE | auto | Driver: auto, ntm, local |
| --parallel=N | 4 | Concurrent sessions |
| --repos=PATTERN | all | Filter repos by pattern |
| --skip-days=N | 7 | Skip recently reviewed |
| --priority=LEVEL | all | Min priority threshold |
| --dry-run | false | Discovery only |
| --resume | false | Resume interrupted |
| --push | false | Allow pushing (with --apply) |
| --max-repos=N | unlimited | Cost budget |
| --max-runtime=MIN | unlimited | Time budget |
| --max-questions=N | unlimited | Question budget |
| --json | false | JSON output |

### Main Dispatch Addition
Add to case statement in main():
```bash
review) cmd_review "$@" ;;
```

### Help Text Addition
Add to show_help():
```
  review       Review GitHub issues and PRs using Claude Code
```

## Testing
- Verify --help includes review command
- Verify unknown args produce error
- Verify prerequisites check runs
- Verify dry-run exits cleanly

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cmd_review() function exists and is callable
- [ ] All arguments parsed correctly
- [ ] --help shows review command
- [ ] --dry-run works (even with stub discovery)
- [ ] Lock acquisition/release works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:52.969404017-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:18:34.751746619-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:18:34.751746619-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented review cmd skeleton + arg routing"}

-{"id":"bd-mrb2","title":"Implement verbose and debug logging modes","description":"Implements --verbose and --debug flags for agent-sweep with detailed operational logging.

## Background

For troubleshooting and development, users need visibility into internal operations beyond the normal progress display. This task implements two levels of detailed output.

## Logging Levels

### --verbose (Level 1)
Shows operational details useful for understanding what is happening:
- ntm command invocations and responses
- Phase transition timing
- File validation decisions
- Commit/release plan contents (summarized)

### --debug (Level 2, implies --verbose)
Shows everything including internal state:
- Full ntm command output
- JSON parsing steps
- State file read/write operations
- Lock acquisition/release
- Full plan JSON before/after validation

## Implementation

### Log Format

```
[agent-sweep] [2024-01-15 10:23:45] [DEBUG] ntm --robot-spawn returned: session_id=as-12345
[agent-sweep] [2024-01-15 10:23:45] [VERBOSE] Phase 1 starting for owner/repo
[agent-sweep] [2024-01-15 10:23:45] [INFO] Processing repo 2 of 5: owner/repo
```

### Logging Functions

```bash
# Log at different levels
log_debug() { [[ $LOG_LEVEL -ge 2 ]] && log_stderr "[DEBUG]" "$*"; }
log_verbose() { [[ $LOG_LEVEL -ge 1 ]] && log_stderr "[VERBOSE]" "$*"; }
log_info() { log_stderr "[INFO]" "$*"; }
log_warn() { log_stderr "[WARN]" "$*"; }
log_error() { log_stderr "[ERROR]" "$*"; }
```

### File Logging

In addition to stderr, write logs to:
- ~/.local/state/ru/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/agent_sweep.log
- Per-repo logs: ~/.local/state/ru/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/repos/<owner>_<repo>.log

## Sensitive Data Handling

- Never log full prompt contents (may contain secrets from repo)
- Redact API keys, tokens, passwords in debug output
- Summarize long JSON rather than dumping full content

## Related Beads

- Works with: bd-qhw3 (progress display)
- Parent epic: bd-kvu5 (Error Handling & Recovery)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --verbose shows ntm interactions and phase transitions
- [ ] --debug shows full internal state
- [ ] Logs written to state directory with date organization
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted from logs
- [ ] Log functions respect global LOG_LEVEL variable
- [ ] Works correctly with parallel mode (no interleaved logs)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:22:42.52168366-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:27:42.312296941-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mrb2","depends_on_id":"bd-kczb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:36.157444768-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-n11g","title":"Fix one-liner installer + flock dependency","description":"## Problem\nThe installer run via: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater/main/install.sh | bash\ncan fail with: 'Could not parse version from GitHub API response' (cached older installer / GitHub API rate limits). Also ru parallel sync currently uses flock for atomic writes, which is missing on some platforms (notably macOS).\n\n## Goals\n- Installer should not require GitHub API to install latest; use releases/latest/download assets and cache-bust internal downloads.\n- Ensure installer docs/behavior clearly support cache-busting (ru_cb query param).\n- Remove hard runtime dependency on system flock by using a portable lock fallback (mkdir/ln lock), or otherwise ensure reliable cross-platform behavior without requiring system package installs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- One-liner installer works reliably without GitHub API.\n- Installer uses cache-busting for its own downloads and provides actionable guidance.\n- Parallel sync atomic results aggregation works on macOS and Linux without requiring flock.\n- ShellCheck passes (severity warning+).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T13:46:06.325794458-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T14:06:33.033656291-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T14:06:33.033656291-05:00","close_reason":"Core fix complete: installer avoids GitHub API, flock replaced with portable dir-based locking. Tests need updating for new lock function names."}
